### Post by crsd36 on 2007-07-21
After following [this thread]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=343670"), the resolution on my laptop has gone haywire.  I only have two options for resolution size:  640x480 and 800x600.  Before this I had many other options.

I have tried going through that thread undoing the steps as I went along but to no avail.

Here's a copy of my /etc/X11/xorg.conf:
```

# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

EndSection

### Post by alienexplorers on 2007-07-21
You could try adding a modeline to your monitor section of the xorg.conf file.  To do this go to:
> [http://www.sh.nu/nvidia/gtf.php](http://www.sh.nu/nvidia/gtf.php)
You should also add your horizontal and verticle refresh rates.

---

### Post by kyphi on 2007-07-21
Curious that your xorg config file states your available resolutions as other than the ones you say you can get.

It would seem that you need to reconfigure that file.

Go to Applications in your desktop menu, then Accessories and then Terminal.

Type ```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
```

Using the up/down keys on your keyboard scroll to the resolution entries that you want and press the spacebar to select.  Then press Enter and then exit.

A reboot is generally required.

If that does not fix it, you may have to reinstall your ATI video driver.

I must admit that I did not follow that very long thread you quoted to its end, so may have missed the point entirely.

---

### Post by alienexplorers on 2007-07-23
What type and model display are you using.  Also, What type of ATI card do you have.  Have you tried using the ENVY Script > [http://www.albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html](http://www.albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html).  It makes setting up the display drivers a piece of cake.

---

### Post by crsd36 on 2007-07-26
Sweet, I'm trying that now.  For the record I'm using a Thinkpad R32 which uses an ATI Mobility M6 video card.  As for the monitor, I'm not sure which one I have...

Thank you for the help and I'll update after I run ENVY

EDIT:  I used ENVY to uninstall and reinstall the ATI driver and things are fixed!  Thank you!
